annotations.label: Outer product (lead)

anchor.snippet: the outer product of two coordinate vectors is the matrix whose entries are all products of an element in the first vector with an element in the second vector

annotations.note: `Matrix.vecMulVec w v i j = w i * v j` is exactly the entrywise outer product of two vectors as a matrix.

annotations.label: Outer product of tensors (lead)

anchor.snippet: given two tensors (multidimensional arrays of numbers), their outer product is a tensor

annotations.note: Mathlib has the abstract tensor product `⊗ₜ` but no concrete entrywise outer product of multidimensional arrays.

annotations.label: Outer product of two vectors

anchor.snippet: their outer product, denoted

annotations.note: `Matrix.vecMulVec` directly defines the outer product `u ⊗ v` of two vectors as a matrix.

annotations.label: Outer product as matrix multiplication

anchor.snippet: is equivalent to a matrix multiplication

annotations.note: `Matrix.vecMulVec_eq` proves `vecMulVec w v = replicateCol ι w * replicateRow ι v`, identifying the outer product with the column-row matrix product.

annotations.label: Outer product example

anchor.snippet: For instance, if

annotations.note: A specific numerical instance of an outer product is not a standalone lemma in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conjugate transpose form

anchor.snippet: For complex vectors, it is often useful to take the conjugate transpose

annotations.note: `Matrix.conjTranspose_vecMulVec` computes `(vecMulVec w v)ᴴ`, capturing the conjugate-transpose form of the outer product.

annotations.label: Dot product as trace of outer product

anchor.snippet: The dot product is the trace of the outer product

annotations.note: `Matrix.trace_vecMulVec` proves `trace (vecMulVec a b) = a ⬝ᵥ b`.

annotations.label: Non-commutativity of outer product

anchor.snippet: Unlike the dot product, the outer product is not commutative

annotations.note: No standalone statement asserting `vecMulVec u v ≠ vecMulVec v u` in general was found.

annotations.label: Vector-matrix multiplication via inner product

anchor.snippet: Multiplication of a vector

annotations.note: `vecMulVec u v *ᵥ w = (v ⬝ᵥ w) • u` (and the dual `vecMul_vecMulVec`) express precisely that an outer-product matrix times a vector collapses to a scalar inner product times the other vector.

annotations.label: Outer product of tensors

anchor.snippet: their outer product

annotations.note: The abstract `⊗ₜ` exists but Mathlib has no entrywise outer product of multi-index arrays as described in the article.

annotations.label: Tensor outer product example

anchor.snippet: For example, if

annotations.note: A specific numerical tensor-outer-product example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Kronecker product as vectorization of outer product

anchor.snippet: the Kronecker product can be viewed as a form of vectorization (or flattening) of the outer product

annotations.note: Mathlib defines both `Matrix.vec` and `Matrix.kroneckerMap` but has no lemma equating `vec(vecMulVec u v)` with a Kronecker product of `u` and `v`.

annotations.label: Matrix product as sum of outer products

anchor.snippet: can be expressed as a sum of column-by-row outer products

annotations.note: The block version `fromCols_mul_fromRows` expresses AB as a sum of block outer products, but the column-by-row decomposition `A * B = ∑ i, vecMulVec (Aᵀ i) (B i)` is not stated.

annotations.label: SVD as sum of outer products

anchor.snippet: the decomposition can be interpreted as the sum of outer products of each left

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `LinearMap.singularValues` but the SVD decomposition as a sum of rank-one outer products is not proved.

annotations.label: Rank-1 decomposition motivates truncated SVD

anchor.snippet: can be expressed as a sum of rank-1 matrices with spectral norm

annotations.note: Truncated SVD and its spectral-norm error bound are not formalized.

annotations.label: Properties of outer product of vectors

anchor.snippet: The outer product of vectors satisfies the following properties

annotations.note: Bilinearity is packaged as `vecMulVecBilin`, with `add_vecMulVec`, `vecMulVec_add`, `smul_vecMulVec`, `transpose_vecMulVec` covering the listed algebraic identities.

annotations.label: Associativity of tensor outer product

anchor.snippet: The outer product of tensors satisfies the additional associativity property

annotations.note: Associativity holds abstractly via `TensorProduct.assoc`, but there is no concrete statement for the entrywise tensor outer product.

annotations.label: Rank of outer product is 1

anchor.snippet: the outer product matrix uv T always has matrix rank 1

annotations.note: `rank_vecMulVec_le` proves `(vecMulVec w v).rank ≤ 1`, matching the rank-≤-1 statement (rank-1 when both vectors are nonzero).

annotations.label: Abstract outer product

anchor.snippet: The outer product of

annotations.note: The abstract outer product `u ⊗ v ∈ V ⊗ W` is exactly `TensorProduct.tmul` (notation `⊗ₜ`).

annotations.label: Outer product as linear map (inner product space)

anchor.snippet: it is possible to define the outer product as a linear map

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace.rankOne 𝕜 x y` is `z ↦ ⟪y, z⟫ • x`, the rank-one operator the article describes.

annotations.label: Outer product in programming languages

anchor.snippet: the outer product, f , of two one-dimensional arrays

annotations.note: Programming-language treatment of outer products is out of scope for Mathlib.

annotations.label: Outer product of complex 2-vectors (spinors)

anchor.snippet: the outer product of these complex 2-vectors is an element of M(2,

annotations.note: The spinor-specific outer-product example is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Determinant of spinor outer product is zero

anchor.snippet: The determinant of this matrix is swtz

annotations.note: `Matrix.det_vecMulVec` shows `(vecMulVec u v).det = 0` whenever the index type has at least two elements, which specializes to the 2×2 spinor case.

annotations.label: Outer product of logical vectors

anchor.snippet: The outer product of two logical vectors

annotations.note: An outer product specialized to Boolean/logical vectors is not formalized in Mathlib.
